pr.title: First bunch of functions of gm_1601.c
pr.author: jurrejelle

summary: Decompiled and typed a first batch of functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1601.c, mostly game-mode/stat/unlock/color-selection helpers, while cleaning up related headers and struct exposure. The PR replaced many placeholder `/// #...` stubs with C implementations, added `NUM_LBL_8046B668`, converted `lbl_8046B668` usage toward an array of `struct lbl_8046B668_t`, and updated prototypes in gm_1601.h plus related forward/type headers. Review focused heavily on using project-preferred integer/bool types, preserving unknown fields as `UNK_T` until understood, simplifying bitmask expressions, and expressing global array access directly instead of via artificial base pointers.
what_changed: Implemented a large first batch of previously stubbed functions in `gm_1601.c`, including stat accumulation/update helpers around `gmMainLib_8015D0C0`, `gmMainLib_8015D1E8`, `gmMainLib_8015D310`, `gmMainLib_8015D06C`, and related accessors.
Added saturating-add style totals that clamp to `(u32)-1`/`-1` in functions such as `gm_801628C4`, `gm_80162C48`, `gm_80162E44`, `gm_80163040`, and `gm_801631F0`.
Implemented boolean update-on-new-record helpers such as `fn_80162BFC` and `fn_80162FF4`, returning true only when a stored stat was increased.
Implemented minimum-over-character/stat helpers such as `gm_80162D6C`, `gm_80162F68`, and `gm_80163164`, iterating over 0x19 entries and starting from minimum value 4.
Implemented stage-mask helpers `gm_80164250` and `fn_801642A0`, using `gmMainLib_8015CC58()->stage_mask` and bit tests over stage indices.
Implemented unlock/selection related code such as `gm_80164A0C`, which searches data near `lbl_803B75F8` and clears a bit in the value returned by `gmMainLib_8015ED8C()`.

Implemented small gate/accessor functions including `gm_8016332C`, `gm_80163690`, `gm_801639F4`, `gm_80167470`, `fn_8016758C`, `gm_801688AC`, `fn_80169444`, `fn_8016A488`, `gm_8016A944`, and `gm_8016A998`.
Implemented color/random assignment routines `fn_801695BC`, `fn_801697FC`, and `fn_8016989C`, using `gm_80169238`, sentinel value `-2`, temporary color arrays, and `HSD_Randi`.
Added `#pragma dont_inline on/reset` around `gm_80169238`, indicating an inlining-sensitive matching constraint.
Changed `gm_8016A92C` to initialize `lbl_8046B668[0].unk1C = -2` and store `&lbl_8046B668` into `StartMeleeRules::x58`.
Changed `gm_8016A98C` to return a pointer to the `lbl_8046B668` array type: `struct lbl_8046B668_t (*)[NUM_LBL_8046B668]`.
Added `#define NUM_LBL_8046B668 27` in `src/melee/gm/forward.h` for the global array length.

Updated prototypes in `gm_1601.h` from `UNK_RET`/generic signatures toward concrete bool, u8, u16, int, long/s32/u32-style signatures, though review comments show some type refinements were still being actively corrected.
Included `mn/types.h` from `gm_1601.h` and adjusted includes in `gm_1601.c`, replacing/removing several unused headers and adding `gmstamina.h` and `gm/forward.h`.
smart_moves: Promoted clear 0-or-1 return functions to `bool` after review, which improves semantic accuracy and was noted by the reviewer as unlikely to cause mismatches when a function cannot return anything other than true/false.

Kept unresolved structure field `StartMeleeRules::x58` as pointer-like/unknown (`UNK_T`) rather than forcing a premature concrete struct type; review explicitly said to use `UNK_T` until fields are determined.
Reused a named array-size macro, `NUM_LBL_8046B668`, and `ARRAY_SIZE(lbl_8046B668)` rather than hardcoding all accesses to the 27-element global array.
Used direct array access for `lbl_8046B668` in `gm_8016A998` after review feedback, avoiding an artificial `base_ptr` variable and making the code more idiomatic.
Simplified the stage mask check to `return (1 << mask) & gmMainLib_8015CC58()->stage_mask;`, matching reviewer preference and making the bit-test intent obvious.
Introduced a `dont_inline` pragma around `gm_80169238`, a targeted matching tactic for a small accessor whose inlining behavior likely affected codegen.
Encoded repeated stat patterns consistently: get per-character slot via `gm_80164024(ckind)`, compare existing value, update only if the new value is larger, and return whether an update occurred.
Separated forward declarations/type ownership into `forward.h`, `types.h`, and `gm_1601.h` rather than keeping all local declarations in the C file/static header.
decomp_lessons: For Melee decomp work, prefer semantic `bool` plus `true`/`false` when the original function only returns 1 or 0; the reviewer stated this kind of change basically never causes a mismatch.
Avoid C `long` in implementation code when the width/sign is intended as a 32-bit signed value; reviewer preference was `s32` over `long`.
Do not over-type unknown pointer fields just because a comparison or cast matches assembly. If the field layout is not known, keep it as `UNK_T` and express pointer checks as `field != NULL`.
Unsignedness can reveal intent: reviewer identified a cast-heavy nonzero test on `x58` as a null check based on unsignedness.
Global arrays should generally be expressed as arrays and indexed directly, not through temporary base-pointer aliases created to satisfy decompiler output.
When a function is a bitmask membership test, a compact expression like `(1 << mask) & stage_mask` is preferred over extra parentheses/temporaries if it still matches.
For array-returning globals, use a precise pointer-to-array return type when the symbol is known as an array, as with `struct lbl_8046B668_t (*gm_8016A98C(void))[NUM_LBL_8046B668]`.
Repeated generated-looking loops can often be matched while still being cleaned up: this PR retained do/while and explicit bounds where needed, but review pushed for clearer types and array forms.
Header prototypes should be updated in tandem with C implementations; otherwise mismatches between `long`, `s32`, `u32`, and `bool` can linger across compilation units.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based names for unidentified functions, e.g. `gm_801628C4`, `fn_80162FF4`, `gm_8016A998`, matching the existing project convention for not-yet-named functions.
Used subsystem prefixes: `gm_` for game-mode functions, `gmMainLib_` for main game-mode library accessors, and `fn_` for local/static or still-unknown functions.
Used `ckind` / `c_kind` to denote character kind arguments where functions call `gm_80164024` for character index normalization.
Used `NUM_LBL_8046B668` as a count macro for the global `lbl_8046B668` array; this follows a symbol-based macro naming style when the semantic name is not yet known.
Preserved unknown field names such as `unk1C`, `unk1D`, `x58`, `x1B8`, and `FighterMatchInfo[i].x8` where field semantics were not fully established.
Reviewer feedback implies project style favors fixed-width typedefs (`s32`, `u32`, `u8`, etc.) over platform C types like `long` when practical.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used saturating overflow checks of the form `(u32)(accum + value) > (u32)-1` to select either `-1`/`(u32)-1` or the computed sum.
Inserted `#pragma dont_inline on` / `#pragma dont_inline reset` around `gm_80169238` to control compiler inlining for matching.
Used pointer arithmetic and casts against `gmMainLib_8015EDBC()` in `gm_80163298`, specifically offset `((temp_r3 * 4) & 0x3FC) + 0x114`, indicating a layout not yet fully expressed as a struct.
Used sentinel byte `-2` in color/character assignment buffers in `fn_801695BC` and related routines, matching surrounding code such as `fn_80169574` that writes `-2` as a terminator.
Used `HSD_Randi` for random permutation/color assignment behavior while preserving loop-heavy control flow in `fn_801695BC`.
Used bit operations for stage/unlock state: `(1 << mask) & stage_mask`, `1 << (u16)var_r29`, and `*temp_r31 &= ~(1ULL << var_r0)`.
Retained explicit loop bounds observed in the diff, including 0x19 for many per-character/stat loops, 0x1D for stage mask scanning, and 0x1B/`ARRAY_SIZE(lbl_8046B668)` for the `lbl_8046B668` table.

Changed `lbl_8046B668` handling from scalar/field-style `lbl_8046B668.unk_1C` toward array-style `lbl_8046B668[0].unk1C` and `&lbl_8046B668[i]`, improving both type correctness and likely codegen clarity.

review_feedback.feedback: Change `x58` to `UNK_T` until its fields are determined; the check should be a pointer null check, `gm_8016AE50()->x58 != NULL`.
`gm_8016A944` should be `bool` because it only returns 1 or 0, with `return true;` and `return false;`.
`fn_80162FF4` should be `bool fn_80162FF4(u8 ckind, u32 arg1)` with boolean returns.
Try direct array access of `lbl_8046B668` without declaring an artificial `base_ptr` in `gm_8016A998`.
Assign `arg0->x58 = &lbl_8046B668;` rather than casting the address through an integer or unnecessary type.

follow_up_queries: Audit `gm_1601.h` prototypes after this PR for remaining `long`/`unsigned long` declarations that should become `s32`/`u32` to match reviewer style.
Determine the real type and field layout behind `StartMeleeRules::x58` and `lbl_8046B668` once more callsites are decompiled.
Search for other functions in `gm_1601.c` using the same stat update/total/min patterns and reuse the boolean update helper style.
Investigate whether offsets into `gmMainLib_8015EDBC()` in `gm_80163298` can be replaced with a named struct field.
Review color assignment functions `fn_801695BC`, `fn_801697FC`, and `fn_8016989C` for possible semantic names once character color selection behavior is understood.
Look for other stage-mask helpers that can use the same simplified `(1 << mask) & stage_mask` expression.
Check if the `dont_inline` pragma around `gm_80169238` remains necessary after future compiler/type cleanup.
